narrative.headline: What 2014 Chevrolet Impala owners report most
narrative.summary: Electrical system faults are the most-reported problem category, with owners describing battery failures that caused physical damage to nearby components. Steering complaints frequently involve loss of vehicle control during cornering, while a notable share of reports describe the StabiliTrak stability-control system triggering autonomously and reducing engine power without driver input. Brake-related complaints overlap with the steering and stability-control accounts, appearing in loss-of-control incidents on slippery roads.

issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: Owners report battery failure accompanied by leakage that damaged adjacent metal components, with some cases requiring multiple repair attempts before the problem was resolved. · 5 fire · 1 crash

issues.title: Steering
issues.blurb: Owners report the vehicle understeering and losing traction entirely during cornering at highway speeds, causing the car to skid toward the outside of a curve without brake input. · 5 crash · 1 injured

issues.title: Electronic Stability Control (Esc)
issues.blurb: Owners report the vehicle slowing on its own at highway speeds while the information center displays a StabiliTrak malfunction message alongside a traction-control warning light and a malfunction indicator light.

issues.title: Service Brakes
issues.blurb: Owners report loss of traction and uncontrolled skidding on slippery curves, with the vehicle decelerating and drifting wide even when brakes were not applied. · 2 fire · 6 crash · 1 injured
